LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT 1030 POLK ASSOCIATES LP'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. ASSUMING PLAINTIFF'S PLEADINGS SATISFY CCP 474, IN ORDER TO DEFEAT "RELATION BACK" OF AN AMENDMENT JOINING "DOE" DEFENDANTS, THE BURDEN IS ON DEFENDANT TO PROVE-EITHER ON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR AT TRIAL-THAT PLAINTIFF KNEW DEFENDANT'S IDENTITY AND THE FACTS GIVING RISE TO DEFENDANT'S LIABILITY. (FULLER V. TUCKER (2000) 84 CAL.APP.4TH 1163, 1173 [EMPHASIS ADDED].) THE ALLEGATIONS CONTAINED IN THE COMPLAINT ARE ACCEPTED AS TRUE WHEN RULING ON A DEMURRER, AND HERE THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT THE TRUE NAMES OF DEFENDANTS SUED AS DOES WERE UNKNOWN TO PLAINTIFF WHEN THE COMPLAINT WAS FILED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976 (302)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-08-2014 Continued To Oct-07-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-20-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Cameron R. Boucher, Esq. of the Law Office of Brenda Cruz Keith, Tel. 415.626.6494, for plaintiff and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling, which was not contested, as follows: DEFENDANT 1030 POLK ASSOCIATES LP's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. Assuming Plaintiff's pleadings satisfy CCP 474, in order to defeat "relation back" of an amendment joining "Doe" Defendants, the burden is on Defendant to prove-either on motion for summary judgment or at trial-that Plaintiff knew Defendant's identity and the facts giving rise to Defendant's liability. (Fuller v. Tucker (2000) 84 Cal.App.4th 1163, 1173 [emphasis added].) The allegations contained in the Complaint are accepted as true when ruling on a Demurrer, and here the Complaint alleges that the true names of Defendants sued as Does were unknown to Plaintiff when the Complaint was filed. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976
